article.title: BYD overtakes Tesla and Kia as the best-selling EV brand in key overseas markets

summary: In early 2026, BYD has surpassed Tesla, Kia, and Volkswagen to become the leading electric vehicle (EV) brand in key overseas markets including the UK, Australia, and Brazil. Despite a global decline in new energy vehicle sales, BYD’s exports surged, driven by affordable models and expanding production plans, signaling a shift in the global EV landscape.
keyPoints: BYD holds over 7% EV market share in the UK for 2026, selling 12,754 electric cars through April.
In April 2026, BYD sold 321,123 new energy vehicles (NEVs), including 156,944 battery EVs and 157,156 plug-in hybrids.
NEV sales dropped 26% year-over-year in April, but BYD’s overseas exports rose 70% to 135,098 vehicles, setting a new monthly record.
BYD led EV sales in Australia in April, with the Sealion 7 topping the market at 1,780 deliveries.
In Brazil, BYD became the first Chinese brand to lead overall vehicle sales, selling 14,911 vehicles in April, narrowly surpassing Volkswagen.
BYD’s affordable models, such as the Dolphin Mini (starting at $24,300 in Brazil), and plug-in hybrid King sedan outsold traditional competitors like the Toyota Corolla.

rewrite.title: BYD Tops EV Sales in UK, Australia, Brazil in 2026
rewrite.body:
BYD has emerged as the top-selling electric vehicle brand in several key overseas markets during the first months of 2026, overtaking established players like Tesla, Kia, and Volkswagen. In the UK, BYD captured over 7% of the EV market share by April, selling 12,754 electric vehicles and becoming the leading EV brand. This growth coincides with a 22% year-over-year expansion in the UK EV market, driven partly by sustained high fuel prices encouraging consumers to switch to electric.

Globally, BYD sold 321,123 new energy vehicles in April 2026, split almost evenly between battery electric vehicles (156,944) and plug-in hybrids (157,156). Although overall NEV sales declined 26% compared to April 2025, BYD’s overseas exports surged 70% year-over-year to 135,098 units, marking a new monthly record and underscoring its successful international expansion.

In Australia, BYD’s Sealion 7 led EV sales in April with 1,780 deliveries, outperforming competitors including Tesla’s Model Y. Meanwhile, in Brazil, BYD made history by becoming the first Chinese automaker to top overall vehicle sales, moving 14,911 units in April—slightly ahead of Volkswagen. The company’s affordable Dolphin Mini electric hatchback, priced around $24,300, ranked among the top 10 best-selling vehicles across all powertrains. Additionally, BYD’s plug-in hybrid King sedan outsold the Toyota Corolla, becoming Brazil’s best-selling sedan for the month.

These milestones highlight BYD’s strategic focus on affordability, diverse EV and PHEV offerings, and plans to scale overseas production. Innovations like its 5-minute Flash Charging technology further position BYD to capitalize on growing global EV demand and challenge incumbent automakers in key international markets.
rewrite.disclaimer: AI-assisted rewrite based on extracted text and discussion signals.
